preferred_openings: ${X} has become my biggest obsession lately...
structural_patterns: Three-act recap: 'What [tool] gave me. / What I built on top of it. / What I'm most proud of.'
Staccato emphasis — short sentences in a row: 'Proper schema. Verified. Validated. Accurate.'
Stat-on-its-own line: 'End-to-end in 54.8 seconds at ~2 cents per run.'
Self-deprecating arc: 'I went in wanting X. I came out with Y.'
Specific scene grounding: '...on the factory floor in Weifang at midnight.'
Aphorism close: 'Solve your own problem — it's almost always someone else's problem too.'
Achievement chain with parenthetical asides: 'Won my first one (Nebius.Build, demoed at NVIDIA GTC), then won DataHub a few weeks later, with a handful of others in between.'
Anaphora for emotional builds: 'You feel it... You're jolted awake... You want it for them. You want it for yourself.'
Quote-then-thesis: paste a quote, follow with a 5-7 word distillation. 'The name and the thesis at once.'
Bilingual flair: drop Chinese characters (紅線) when culturally relevant, never as decoration
punctuation_signature: Em dash '—' (not hyphen) for asides and emphasis
Bold labels in markdown lists for technical breakdowns
topical_anchors_observed: AI agents and agent infrastructure (OpenClaw, Elias, multi-agent systems)
Hardware NPI, factory ops, cross-continental manufacturing
Short-term rental portfolio operations and data quality
Hackathons, building in public, weekend tinkering
Data lineage, metadata catalogs, fine-tuning, embeddings
Bilingual EN/Mandarin work in Asian factory contexts
Hospitality and design — brand-as-build, sense of place, Rosewood-class craft
Ikigai philosophy and purpose-chasing — the emotional/aspirational register
Active builds: injester (data lineage / catalog), Agent Web Protocol (open agent interop), LaClawClaw (commerce agents)
Compressed thesis frame: 'agents × commerce × open protocols' — three-noun-cross product naming
